title: Pilot risk and open-question review
expected_decisions: Do not announce a final pilot launch date until pricing approval and the security review are complete.
Do not promise single sign-on or sixty-minute meeting support until the client confirms the requirements and the additional scope is approved.

expected_actions.action: Obtain written pricing approval and circulate the approved pricing table to the team.
Send the completed security review summary covering storage access, administrator permissions, and deletion controls.

expected_risks: Delayed pricing approval could postpone the client follow-up and prevent confirmation of the commercial pilot.
Unclear data-retention requirements could delay legal and privacy approval.
The unconfirmed pilot user count could create inaccurate capacity and pricing assumptions.
An incomplete security review could delay the planned pilot launch.
Low-quality or unsupported recordings could cause processing failures or incomplete meeting notes.
expected_open_questions: Whether the client requires single sign-on during the pilot.
Whether recordings must remain in a specific geographic region.
Whether external contractors will be included in the pilot.
Whether sixty-minute recordings must be supported in the initial pilot.
must_not_invent: A commitment to support sixty-minute meetings
audio_notes: Synthetic multi-speaker risk review at natural speech speed with 7.5-second pauses between turns.
